title: veridict power report
description: JSON output of `veridict power`. Report-only, a pure calculation from flags - except --metric mean-diff --pilot FILE, which reads real pilot data to estimate a standard deviation. Estimates how many trials compare would need for a target_power probability of reaching a passing verdict, assuming the true effect is exactly assume_effect against a pass bar of min_effect. winrate/sign-test/elo use an exact binomial search; mean-diff uses a closed-form normal approximation given an assumed/estimated standard deviation (assume_sd/sd_source).

schema_version.description: Stays 1 across additive changes; bumps only when a field is removed or renamed.

ci_method.description: Always wilson for metric elo (the same restriction compare --metric elo itself has); always "normal" for mean-diff, signaling the structurally different closed-form-normal-approximation method rather than a real CI-method choice.

min_effect.description: The pass bar - identical meaning to compare --min-effect/--pass-above.

assume_effect.description: The true effect being powered for. Strictly greater than min_effect - evaluating power with the true effect equal to the pass bar only recovers the interval's own miscoverage at that boundary, not a number that climbs toward target_power with more trials.

target_power.description: Target probability of reaching a passing verdict, assuming the true effect is exactly assume_effect.

achieved_power.description: The real exact power at estimated_trials, always >= target_power - can overshoot the target by a nontrivial margin due to the discrete, non-monotonic ('sawtooth') nature of exact CI methods; see docs/metrics.md's power section.

notes.description: Always includes the 'assumes the true effect is exactly assume_effect' caveat; adds a --paired-by-id caveat when that flag was set. For mean-diff, always includes the normal-approximation and assume_sd-is-the-difference's-SD caveats, plus a tiny-pilot caveat when --pilot yielded fewer than 30 diffs.

assume_sd.description: mean-diff only: the assumed standard deviation of the paired difference (candidate - baseline), from --assume-sd or estimated from --pilot data. Omitted for every other metric.

sd_source.description: mean-diff only: which source assume_sd came from. Omitted for every other metric.
